question: Which gas is the most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?

sources.title: The Ozone Layer
sources.text: The ozone layer in the stratosphere absorbs most of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet radiation, protecting life on the surface.

sources.title: Composition of Air
sources.text: Nitrogen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere, making up the largest share of the air by volume.

mock.claim: Nitrogen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere.
mock.output: Nitrogen is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere [S1].

expected: The claim is true and supported by S2 (Composition of Air); the citation should point to S2, not S1.
why: The cited source S1 is about the ozone layer and never mentions nitrogen; the supporting passage is the uncited S2.
